skill.name: uniprot-database
skill.description: Direct REST API access to UniProt for protein search, entry retrieval, and identifier mapping; use when you need programmatic UniProtKB queries or cross-database ID conversion.
skill.summary: Python client for the UniProt REST API to search, retrieve, and map protein data programmatically.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 82 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified markdown backtick formatting in .md files as Ruby/shell command execution (51 locations). Legitimate Python requests to the official UniProt REST API (rest.uniprot.org) were flagged as network concerns (18 locations). No prompt injection, credential access, obfuscation, or malicious intent was detected. This is a legitimate bioinformatics skill for querying the UniProt protein database. A prior audit confirms Production Ready grade (90/100) with all veto gates passing.

low_findings.title: Markdown code formatting misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged inline markdown code backticks (e.g., `gene:BRCA1`, `primaryAccession`) as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All occurrences are markdown formatting in .md documentation files. No actual shell execution commands exist in the skill.

low_findings.title: Legitimate UniProt API network requests
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged Python requests library calls to rest.uniprot.org as suspicious network activity. These are the core intended functionality of this bioinformatics skill. All requests target the official UniProt REST API for protein data retrieval. No data exfiltration or unauthorized endpoints detected.

low_findings.title: False positive weak cryptographic algorithm detection
low_findings.description: Scanner detected 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at locations containing normal code. Affected lines include argparse.ArgumentParser setup, JSON field names like proteinDescription, and code example comments. No actual cryptographic operations exist in the skill.

content.user_title: Query the UniProt Protein Database via REST API
content.value_statement: Researchers need programmatic access to UniProt protein data without managing complex SDKs. This skill provides direct Python-based REST API access for protein search, entry retrieval, and identifier mapping.
actual_capabilities: Search UniProtKB using Lucene-style queries by gene name, organism, or protein name.
Retrieve detailed protein entries by UniProt accession number in multiple formats.
Map identifiers between biological databases such as gene names and Ensembl IDs.
Submit and poll asynchronous identifier mapping jobs until results are ready.
Run as a standalone Python CLI tool with search, retrieve, and map subcommands.
Integrate protein data retrieval into automated analysis pipelines.
limitations: Read-only access to UniProt; cannot submit or modify protein entries.
Limited to databases supported by the UniProt ID mapping service.
No built-in caching for repeated queries across multiple sessions.
Simplified synchronous wrapper does not handle concurrent mapping jobs.

use_cases.title: Protein Function Research
use_cases.description: Search and retrieve detailed protein entries for functional annotation and sequence analysis using the UniProtKB database.

use_cases.title: Cross-Reference Database Linking
use_cases.description: Map identifiers across biological databases such as gene names, Ensembl, and RefSeq to UniProt accessions for integrated data analysis.

use_cases.title: Pipeline Automation
use_cases.description: Integrate automated protein data retrieval into continuous analysis pipelines using the Python CLI or library interface.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Protein Search
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner wants to search for a protein by gene name and organism.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the UniProt database for human BRCA1 protein and return the first 3 results with accession numbers and protein names in JSON format.

prompt_templates.title: Entry Retrieval by Accession
prompt_templates.scenario: User knows a specific UniProt accession and wants the full record.
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve the full UniProt entry for accession P38398 and display the primary accession, protein name, gene names, and organism information.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Database Identifier Mapping
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs to map gene names to UniProtKB accessions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Map the gene names BRCA1 and TP53 to UniProtKB accessions using the UniProt ID mapping service and show the results.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Protein Analysis Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: An experienced user builds an automated pipeline for batch protein analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Python script that searches for all reviewed human kinases in UniProt, retrieves their amino acid sequences, and maps each entry to its corresponding Ensembl gene ID. Use format control for consistent JSON parsing throughout.

output_examples.input: Search UniProt for human insulin protein (gene:INS, organism_id:9606) with format JSON.
output: The skill returns a JSON object containing matching UniProt entries. Each result includes the primaryAccession (e.g., P01308 for insulin), the protein name and synonyms, gene names, organism taxonomy data, and cross-reference links. The results array is sorted by relevance by the UniProt search engine.

For the insulin search, the first result shows accession P01308 with protein name 'Insulin', gene name 'INS', and organism 'Homo sapiens'. Additional metadata includes sequence length, mass, and functional annotations.

output_examples.input: Retrieve the full entry for UniProt accession P38398 (human BRCA1) in JSON format.
output: The entry response includes the primary accession P38398, UniProt ID 'BRCA1_HUMAN', the recommended protein name 'Breast cancer type 1 susceptibility protein', gene names including BRCA1, the organism 'Homo sapiens' with taxonomy ID 9606, and the full amino acid sequence with length and molecular weight.
Additional fields include functional descriptions, subcellular location, domains, and cross-references to other databases like PubMed, Ensembl, and PDB.

best_practices: Always specify the output format parameter (json, tsv, or fasta) for consistent and predictable parsing of results.
Use specific query fields like gene: or organism_id: with the AND operator to narrow search results and avoid large response sets.
Implement error handling for HTTP failures and API rate limits when using the skill in production pipelines.
anti_patterns: Do not send unsanitized user input directly as query parameters without validation or encoding.
Avoid polling the UniProt ID mapping status endpoint more than once per second to respect service limits.
Do not rely on wildcard queries alone without additional filters, as this can return excessive results and cause timeouts.

faq.question: What is UniProt?
faq.answer: UniProt is a comprehensive protein database that provides protein sequence, functional information, and cross-references to other biological databases.

faq.question: What is a UniProt accession?
faq.answer: A unique identifier assigned to each protein entry in UniProtKB. For example, P38398 is the accession for the human BRCA1 protein.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without Python?
faq.answer: Yes. The Python code is provided as a reference implementation. You can use any HTTP client to call the UniProt REST API directly.

faq.question: What is identifier mapping?
faq.answer: It converts identifiers between different biological databases. For example, you can map a gene name like BRCA1 to its UniProtKB accession.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: JSON, TSV, FASTA, and plain text are supported through the UniProt REST API format parameter.

faq.question: Is there a rate limit for the UniProt API?
faq.answer: UniProt requests reasonable usage. Avoid sending more than 100 requests per second to the REST API endpoints.
